title_en: Importance for Students and Available Editions
content_en:
The Wafayat al-A'yan is essential reading for anyone studying Islamic civilization in its classical period. Its cultural breadth makes it relevant across an unusually wide range of specializations: students of Islamic law, hadith, theology, literature, medicine, philosophy, history, and the arts will all find valuable material. The work functions as an encyclopedia of Islamic civilization rather than a narrow specialist text, and consulting it regularly while studying any aspect of classical Islamic culture deepens understanding and provides context.

For students of Arabic literature, the extensive verse quotations throughout the work make it both an anthology of classical Arabic poetry and a guide to the lives of major poets. Ibn Khallikan's critical observations about poetic quality and his accounts of the circumstances in which poems were composed illuminate the social dimensions of Arabic literary culture in ways that reading the poetry in isolation cannot.

For students of the Islamic sciences — hadith, fiqh, tafsir, kalam — the Wafayat provides human faces and biographical contexts for figures who often appear in other sources merely as names in chains of transmission. Understanding that a particular hadith scholar lived in a specific city, studied under certain teachers, and worked within particular political circumstances brings the transmission chains to life and aids memory and understanding.

For researchers in Islamic history and civilization, the work is a primary source of the highest importance. It is regularly cited in scholarly articles and monographs as the source for biographical data about medieval Islamic figures, and any serious researcher in pre-modern Islamic history needs to be able to use it.

The standard modern edition of the Wafayat al-A'yan is the eight-volume set edited by Ihsan Abbas and published by Dar Sadir in Beirut. This edition is carefully prepared, includes helpful indexes of names and places, and is widely available. Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah in Beirut has also published the work in multiple formats. De Slane's French translation (1843–1871), while dated, remains valuable for its extensive notes and is available in major research libraries. An abridged English translation by Olivia Remie Constable of selected entries appears in her sourcebook on medieval Iberian history, but no complete English translation exists. Students without Arabic will find the secondary literature helpful: Hamilton Gibb's classic studies of Islamic biography and numerous articles in the Encyclopaedia of Islam draw heavily on the Wafayat and can guide those seeking specific information.
title_id: Pentingnya bagi Para Pelajar dan Edisi yang Tersedia
content_id:
Wafayat al-A'yan merupakan bacaan wajib bagi siapa pun yang mempelajari peradaban Islam pada periode klasiknya. Keluasan budayanya menjadikannya relevan di berbagai spesialisasi yang sangat luas: mahasiswa hukum Islam, hadis, teologi, sastra, kedokteran, filsafat, sejarah, dan seni semua akan menemukan materi yang berharga. Karya ini berfungsi sebagai ensiklopedia peradaban Islam daripada teks spesialis yang sempit.

Bagi mahasiswa sastra Arab, kutipan-kutipan puisi ekstensif di seluruh karya menjadikannya baik antologi puisi Arab klasik maupun panduan tentang kehidupan penyair-penyair utama. Pengamatan-pengamatan kritis Ibn Khallikan tentang kualitas puisi menerangi dimensi-dimensi sosial budaya sastra Arab.

Bagi mahasiswa ilmu-ilmu Islam, Wafayat memberikan wajah-wajah manusia dan konteks-konteks biografis bagi tokoh-tokoh yang dalam sumber-sumber lain sering muncul sekadar sebagai nama-nama dalam rantai transmisi. Memahami bahwa seorang ulama hadis tertentu tinggal di kota tertentu dan belajar di bawah guru-guru tertentu menghidupkan rantai-rantai transmisi dan membantu pemahaman.

Bagi peneliti dalam sejarah dan peradaban Islam, karya ini adalah sumber primer dengan kepentingan tertinggi. Ia secara teratur dikutip dalam artikel-artikel dan monografi-monografi keilmuan sebagai sumber data biografis tentang tokoh-tokoh Islam abad pertengahan.

Edisi modern standar dari Wafayat al-A'yan adalah set delapan volume yang disunting oleh Ihsan Abbas dan diterbitkan oleh Dar Shadir di Beirut. Edisi ini disiapkan dengan cermat, mencakup indeks-indeks nama dan tempat yang berguna, dan tersedia secara luas. Terjemahan Prancis de Slane (1843-1871), meskipun sudah usang, tetap berharga karena catatan-catatannya yang ekstensif dan tersedia di perpustakaan-perpustakaan penelitian utama.
